description: Cross-framework mapping from SFDR mandatory PAI indicators to GRI Standards 2021 disclosures
notes: Mapping between the 18 mandatory PAI indicators under SFDR RTS (Delegated Regulation EU 2022/1288) and equivalent or related GRI disclosures. PAI indicators are investor-level metrics (portfolio aggregation), while GRI disclosures are entity-level. The mapping identifies the underlying entity-level GRI data that feeds into PAI calculations.

mappings.source_name: GHG emissions (Scope 1, 2, 3, Total)
mappings.notes: GRI 305-1 provides the entity-level Scope 1 emissions data needed for PAI 1. Both require GHG Protocol methodology and CO2e units.

mappings.source_name: GHG emissions (Scope 1, 2, 3, Total)
mappings.notes: GRI 305-2 provides entity-level Scope 2 emissions. GRI requires both location-based and market-based; PAI uses location-based as primary.

mappings.source_name: GHG emissions (Scope 1, 2, 3, Total)
mappings.notes: GRI 305-3 provides entity-level Scope 3 emissions across 15 categories. PAI requires total Scope 3; GRI may report by category.

mappings.source_name: Carbon footprint
mappings.notes: PAI 2 normalises total GHG by portfolio value (investor metric). GRI 305-1/2/3 provide entity-level numerator data. No direct GRI equivalent for portfolio-level carbon footprint.

mappings.source_name: Carbon footprint
mappings.notes: Scope 2 component of the carbon footprint numerator.

mappings.source_name: GHG intensity of investee companies
mappings.notes: GRI 305-4 requires entity-level GHG intensity per chosen denominator (revenue, production, etc.). PAI 3 uses revenue as denominator. Direct match when GRI uses revenue.

mappings.source_name: Exposure to companies active in the fossil fuel sector
mappings.target_name: Direct economic value generated and distributed
mappings.notes: No direct GRI equivalent for fossil fuel activity classification. GRI 201-1 revenue breakdown can help identify fossil fuel revenue. Sector-specific GRI/SASB disclosures more directly relevant.

mappings.source_name: Share of non-renewable energy consumption and production
mappings.notes: GRI 302-1 requires breakdown of energy consumption by fuel type (renewable vs non-renewable). Provides the entity-level data needed for PAI 5.

mappings.source_name: Energy consumption intensity per high impact climate sector
mappings.notes: GRI 302-3 requires entity-level energy intensity ratio. PAI 6 adds sector segmentation by NACE code. The entity-level intensity from GRI feeds directly into PAI 6 calculation.

mappings.source_name: Activities negatively affecting biodiversity-sensitive areas
mappings.target_name: Operational sites owned, leased, managed in, or adjacent to, protected areas and areas of high biodiversity value outside protected areas
mappings.notes: GRI 304-1 identifies sites near protected/biodiversity-sensitive areas. PAI 7 further requires assessment of negative impact. GRI 304-2 on significant impacts complements.

mappings.source_name: Activities negatively affecting biodiversity-sensitive areas
mappings.target_name: Significant impacts of activities, products and services on biodiversity
mappings.notes: GRI 304-2 describes significant impacts on biodiversity — directly relevant to the 'negatively affecting' criterion in PAI 7.

mappings.source_name: Emissions to water
mappings.notes: GRI 303-4 covers water discharge including quality. PAI 8 specifically targets emissions of priority substances to water (per Water Framework Directive). GRI 303-4 is broader and may not itemise priority substances specifically.

mappings.source_name: Emissions to water
mappings.target_name: Waste generation and significant waste-related impacts
mappings.notes: GRI 306 series covers waste and effluents. GRI 306-1 describes waste-related impacts that may include water emissions, but is not a direct match for PAI 8 priority substance emissions.

mappings.source_name: Hazardous waste and radioactive waste ratio
mappings.notes: GRI 306-3 requires reporting total waste by composition and by hazardous/non-hazardous. Provides the entity-level hazardous waste data needed for PAI 9.

mappings.source_name: Hazardous waste and radioactive waste ratio
mappings.notes: GRI 306-4 breaks down hazardous and non-hazardous waste diverted from disposal. Provides supplementary detail for PAI 9.

mappings.source_name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines
mappings.notes: GRI 2-27 covers compliance with laws and regulations broadly. PAI 10 is specifically about UNGC/OECD violations. GRI 2-27 would capture some but not all relevant violations.

mappings.source_name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines
mappings.target_name: Incidents of discrimination and corrective actions taken
mappings.notes: Discrimination incidents may constitute UNGC Principle 6 violations. GRI 406-1 provides entity-level data on one dimension of UNGC compliance.

mappings.source_name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines
mappings.target_name: Operations and suppliers at significant risk for incidents of child labour
mappings.notes: Child labour incidents relate to UNGC Principle 5. GRI 408-1 provides relevant data for one dimension of the UNGC violation assessment.

mappings.source_name: Violations of UN Global Compact principles and OECD Guidelines
mappings.target_name: Operations and suppliers at significant risk for incidents of forced or compulsory labour
mappings.notes: Forced labour incidents relate to UNGC Principle 4. GRI 409-1 provides relevant data.

mappings.source_name: Lack of processes and compliance mechanisms to monitor UNGC/OECD compliance
mappings.notes: GRI 2-23 requires disclosure of policy commitments for responsible business conduct, including references to UNGC and OECD Guidelines. PAI 11 specifically asks whether policies/mechanisms exist.

mappings.source_name: Lack of processes and compliance mechanisms to monitor UNGC/OECD compliance
mappings.notes: GRI 2-25 covers grievance mechanisms and remediation processes. PAI 11 asks specifically about grievance/complaints handling mechanisms for UNGC/OECD violations.

mappings.source_name: Lack of processes and compliance mechanisms to monitor UNGC/OECD compliance
mappings.target_name: Mechanisms for seeking advice and raising concerns
mappings.notes: GRI 2-26 covers mechanisms for raising concerns (whistleblowing). Relevant to the grievance mechanism aspect of PAI 11.

mappings.source_name: Unadjusted gender pay gap
mappings.target_name: Ratio of basic salary and remuneration of women to men
mappings.notes: GRI 405-2 requires disclosure of ratio of basic salary and remuneration of women to men by employee category and significant locations of operation. Directly maps to the entity-level data needed for PAI 12, though GRI uses ratio while PAI uses gap percentage.

mappings.source_name: Board gender diversity
mappings.target_name: Diversity of governance bodies and employees
mappings.notes: GRI 405-1 requires disclosure of gender composition of governance bodies. Directly provides the entity-level board gender diversity data needed for PAI 13.

mappings.source_name: Exposure to controversial weapons
mappings.target_name: Activities, value chain and other business relationships
mappings.notes: No direct GRI equivalent for controversial weapons involvement. GRI 2-6 on activities and value chain may provide some context. Assessment typically relies on specialised ESG data providers and screening tools rather than GRI disclosures.

mappings.source_name: GHG intensity (sovereign)
mappings.notes: GRI Standards apply to organizations, not sovereign entities. PAI 15 uses country-level GHG/GDP data from UNFCCC, IEA, or World Bank. No GRI mapping applicable.

mappings.source_name: Investee countries subject to social violations (sovereign)
mappings.notes: GRI Standards apply to organizations, not sovereign entities. PAI 16 uses country-level human rights assessments. No GRI mapping applicable.

mappings.source_name: Exposure to fossil fuels through real estate assets
mappings.notes: No direct GRI equivalent for real estate fossil fuel involvement. GRI 302-1 energy consumption data may indicate fossil fuel use in real estate operations. PAI 17 is specifically about the use/function of the real estate asset.

mappings.source_name: Exposure to energy-inefficient real estate assets
mappings.notes: GRI 302-3 energy intensity could be reported per square meter for real estate. PAI 18 uses EPC ratings (A-G) or top 30% energy consumption threshold. GRI provides underlying energy data but not the EPC classification.
